title: selago corymbosa
intro: Selago corymbosa, also known as blue-flowered or blue-thimbleweed, is a flowering plant species in the family Pedicularis, native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Selago corymbosa is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and it may also be used in the form of tinctures or capsules to leverage its medicinal properties.
